Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Simone Richter" or "BrightLoop apartment" or "studio" (or similar) to find the apartment discrepancy thread. Expected findings: Simone Richter's complaint email (simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) reporting she was placed in a studio instead of the promised one-bedroom, Mina's April 9 reply promising an answer by end of week, Chloe Vance's ops gaps email listing Simone's housing trace as an open item, and Julian Brooks' apology email to Simone acknowledging the missed follow-up.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "UrbanNest" or "Carmen Reyes" or "Simone booking" (or similar) to find whether UrbanNest responded about Simone's apartment booking. Expected findings: an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) requesting the original booking request, confirmation records, and any substitution notes for Simone's placement, sent around April 23.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or "car shipment" (or similar) to find the car delay thread. Expected findings: Road Runner dispatch email (dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com) sent to Blessing Okafor on April 11 reporting Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic is at the Indianapolis transfer hub with a revised delivery window of April 18-20 due to a driver shortage. Also find Marcus Webb's two follow-up emails to support@moveops.com asking for status (email_email_ca010e9c9446 and email_email_87f575fcacf9), and Julian's reply to Marcus promising an update by 2:00 PM Pacific.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "StormCloud" or "goodwill credit" or "$500" or "Liam Park" (or similar) to find the credit investigation thread. Expected findings: Liam Park's escalation email (liam.park@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) listing multiple unresolved issues including the credit never appearing on the invoice. Julian's email to Mina confirming he approved the $500 goodwill credit after the flight issue and noting the credit was entered against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319. Marcus Thorne's reply confirming the posting error from the finance side.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Emilia Cruz" or "NorthWind" or "Steinway" or "piano" (or similar) to find the piano damage thread and any follow-up activity. Expected findings: Craig Nguyen's damage report email (craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com) with extraction notes and photos from the April 11 move, Pam Kowalski's final warning to David Chen citing the piano damage and lack of timely follow-through as one of two incidents damaging NorthWind's confidence.
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "BrightLoop" or "Simone" or "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the BrightLoop service recovery. Expected findings: Julian's April 24 message in #customer-support (C007) announcing he is taking over the BrightLoop service recovery for both employees, Julian's follow-up confirming he sent direct replies to Marcus (Road Runner delay context) and Simone (missed April 8 follow-up apology), and Blessing Okafor's admission that she missed the April 11 Road Runner delay notice.
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "StormCloud" or "credit" or "EST-2026" or "INV-2026" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the StormCloud recovery and credit issue. Expected findings: Marcus Thorne's post in #finance (C005) confirming the $500 credit was applied to estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of invoice INV-2026-0319. Mina's reconstruction thread and recovery plan discussion. Mina's request to Julian for support-history context.
OE 8: Search CRM for BrightLoop using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop") to find the company record, then crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_brightloop") and crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_brightloop"]) to find deal status and engagement notes. Expected findings: deal "BrightLoop Analytics, April 2026 Relocations (Simone Richter + Marcus Webb)" at closedwon stage, amount $11,350. Engagement note (engagement_brightloop_apr2026_relocations) detailing Simone's rush move and Marcus's vehicle shipping with Road Runner (VIN 2HGFC2F53KH123456), invoice INV-2026-0308 for $11,350.
OE 9: Search CRM for NorthWind using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") and crm_list_engagements to find the Emilia Cruz call log. Expected finding: engagement crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c, Julian Brooks' inbound call log from April 12, documenting Emilia Cruz's frustration about the Steinway scratch, her question "Is MoveOps actually taking responsibility here or am I going to get bounced between vendors?", Julian's promise of same-day follow-up, and critically: "No owner assigned during call log entry", confirming nobody was assigned to follow up.
OE 10: Search Linear for BrightLoop issues using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or similar). Expected findings: Issue #412 (linear_issue_c16357d188c6), "BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion", assigned to Mina Hashimoto, priority 1, describing Simone's studio complaint and Marcus's Road Runner delay. Issue #417 (linear_issue_f85be674c9b8), "Document BrightLoop ops gaps", assigned to Chloe Vance, covering Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, and other gaps. Search comments using linear_list_comments for Julian's service recovery updates.
OE 11: Search Linear for StormCloud issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or similar). Expected finding: Issue #284 (linear_issue_a49b4d199139), "StormCloud account incident reconstruction, Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop", assigned to Mina Hashimoto, priority 1. Search comments using linear_list_comments to find Julian's comment (linear_comment_65243eedc4dc) providing credit history, he approved the $500 goodwill credit, it was entered against EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319. Also find Marcus Thorne's comment (linear_comment_1f5055f550ea) confirming the posting error from the finance side and asking Julian to add original support-recovery context.
OE 12: Search Linear for NorthWind issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or similar). Expected finding: Issue #482 (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1), "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", assigned to David Chen, which mentions Julian's support note showing Emilia Cruz requested a same-day callback and no owner picked it up.
OE 13: Search Airtable for BrightLoop relocation records using airtable_list_bases then airtable_list_tables to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01), then airtable_search_records (field_name: "Company", value: "BrightLoop" or similar) or airtable_search_records for "Simone Richter" and "Marcus Webb". Expected findings: Record recSimoneRichterBrightloop, Simone Richter, Chicago to Boston, In Progress, Suki Patel coordinator, rush/lease end April 6, UrbanNest temp housing. Record recMarcusWebbBrightloop, Marcus Webb, Atlanta to Boston, In Progress, Suki Patel coordinator, Honda Civic VIN 2HGFC2F53KH123456, Road Runner add-on ~$1,100.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoices using quickbooks_search_customers (criteria for "StormCloud") to find customer cust_stormcloud, then quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for that customer). Expected finding: Invoice INV-2026-002 for $26,400 (3 relocations at $8,800 each). Note: the document numbers EST-2026-0287 and INV-2026-0319 referenced in the credit mis-posting are discussed in email and Linear threads but may not appear as separate QuickBooks rows, the credit mis-posting evidence comes from the email and Linear investigation in OEs 4, 7, and 11.
OE 15: Look up contact details for Simone Richter using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Simone Richter"). Note: contacts.contacts.json returns simone.richter@stormcloud.io (a different Simone at StormCloud). The correct BrightLoop email (simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com) is found in CRM contacts via crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Simone Richter") or from the email threads and Airtable records discovered in OEs 1 and 13.
OE 16: Look up contact details for Marcus Webb using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Webb"). Expected finding: marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com. Note: multiple people named Marcus Webb exist in the universe. The contacts service returns both a BrightLoop Senior Analyst 1 and an Ironclad Cybersecurity VP of Talent (m.webb@ironcladsec.com). Additionally, CRM has a third Marcus Webb at Canopy Health (Lab Research Associate, marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com). The agent must identify the correct BrightLoop employee.
OE 17: Look up contact details for Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Expected finding: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 18: Look up contact details for Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance"). Expected finding: chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 19: Find the #operations Slack channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Expected finding: channel C006 (operations).
OE 20: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com with an honest status update on the apartment situation, what was found about the studio vs one-bedroom booking discrepancy, whether UrbanNest has responded, and what next steps are.
OE 21: Send email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com with an honest status update on the car shipment, the Road Runner delay (Indianapolis hub), the revised delivery window, and when Marcus can expect a concrete ETA.
OE 22: Send email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with the support-side history for the StormCloud recovery response. Content should include: Julian approved the $500 goodwill credit after the flight issue, the credit was entered against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, the Emilia Cruz NorthWind callback that went unanswered, and any other relevant support-side context Mina needs for her response to CFO Liam Park.
OE 23: Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to the BrightLoop tracking issue (linear_issue_c16357d188c6 #412 or linear_issue_f85be674c9b8 #417) documenting the current status of the Simone apartment and Marcus Webb car recovery, what vendor responses have been received, what was communicated to the employees, and what remains unresolved.
OE 24: Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to the StormCloud reconstruction issue (linear_issue_a49b4d199139 #284) documenting the credit investigation findings, the $500 was posted to EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319, and the current status of the correction.
OE 25: Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to the NorthWind retention issue (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 #482) or create a new issue (via linear_create_issue) documenting that the Emilia Cruz same-day callback promised by Julian on April 12 was never delivered, no owner was assigned and no follow-up occurred.
OE 26: Post a message (via conversations_add_message) in the #operations channel (channel_id: C006) from Julian flagging any vendor or housing items that still need operational follow-through, specifically the UrbanNest apartment resolution for Simone Richter and the Road Runner car delivery status for Marcus Webb, plus any other unresolved operational threads discovered during the investigation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Simone Richter" or "BrightLoop apartment" or "studio" (or similar) to find the apartment discrepancy thread.
